steps.title: Connect snow to carbon output
steps.content: The conclusion says net CO2 is likely to increase when snow cover arrives before the soil surface freezes. A supporting finding should explain why earlier snow would allow more CO2 output than plant CO2 absorption.

steps.title: Choose the insulation effect
steps.content: A discusses solar radiation but not the timing of snow and freezing. B says early snow lowers respiration, which would not support increased CO2. C compares places rather than explaining the timing effect. Snow insulation that lets respiration continue while plants are not growing supports the conclusion. The answer is D.
